perturbation.original_text: The Agent's aggregate fee for its services hereunder will be an amount equal to 3.15% of the gross proceeds from the sale of the Offered Shares sold to Purchasers that are not affiliates of the Agent (such fee payable by the Company at and subject to the consummation of Settlement).
perturbation.changed_text: The Transaction Entities will furnish the Agent with such conformed copies of such opinions, certificates, letters and documents as the Agent reasonably request. The Agent may in its sole discretion waive compliance with any conditions to the obligations of the Agent hereunder. The Agent's aggregate fee for its services hereunder will be an amount equal to 3.15% of the gross proceeds from the sale of the Offered Shares sold to Purchasers that are not affiliates of the Agent (such fee payable by the Company at and subject to the consummation of Settlement).
perturbation.explanation: Moving the Agent's fee disclosure from the section detailing the sale and delivery of the shares to a section about furnishing documents obscures a key financial term of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 15 U.S. Code § 77j - Information required in prospectus
perturbation.law_explanation: This section of the Securities Act requires a prospectus to contain certain financial information, including the underwriter's discounts or commissions, which is essential for investors to evaluate the offering. Moving the fee disclosure to a less prominent location undermines this requirement.

Information required in prospectus (a) Information in registration statement; documents not required Except to the extent otherwise permitted or required pursuant to this subsection or subsections (c), (d), or (e)- (1) a prospectus relating to a security other than a security issued by a foreign government or political subdivision thereof, shall contain the information contained in the regis
perturbation.justification: The altered contract contradicts securities law. The Perturbation Explanation states that moving the agent's fee "obscures a key financial term of the agreement." The Law Explanation states that the Securities Act requires a prospectus to contain financial information such as underwriter's commissions. The original text clearly places the agent's fee within the context of the sale of shares, whereas the changed text buries it within a section about document furnishing. This hinders investors from easily evaluating the offering, violating 15 USC 77j, which requires a prospectus to contain required information.

perturbation.original_text: The Company acknowledges and agrees that (i) there can be no assurance that the Agent will be successful in selling the Offered Shares, and (ii) the Agent will incur no liability or obligation to the Company or any other person or entity if it does not sell the Offered Shares for any reason other than a failure by the Agent to use its commercially reasonable efforts consistent with its normal trading and sales practices to sell such Offered Shares as required herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. Headings. The headings herein are inserted for convenience of reference only and are not intended to be part of, or to affect the meaning or interpretation of, this Agreement. The Company acknowledges and agrees that (i) there can be no assurance that the Agent will be successful in selling the Offered Shares, and (ii) the Agent will incur no liability or obligation to the Company or any other person or entity if it does not sell the Offered Shares for any reason other than a failure by the Agent to use its commercially reasonable efforts consistent with its normal trading and sales practices to sell such Offered Shares as required herein.
perturbation.explanation: The shift of 'no assurance' clause into 'headings' introduces obscurity. It is not where readers would look to determine agent liability.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 17 CFR § 230.421 - Presentation of information in prospectuses
perturbation.law_explanation: This SEC regulation dictates that prospectuses must present information in a clear, concise, and understandable manner. Embedding a key disclaimer about the agent's liability within a section about headings violates this principle by obscuring its relevance and potential impact on investors.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 230.421 - Presentation of information in prospectuses. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 230.421 - Presentation of information in prospectuses. CFR prev | next § 230.421 Presentation of information in prospectuses. (a) The information required in a prospectus need not follow the order of the items or other requirements in the form. Such information shall not, however, be set forth in such fashion as to obscure any of the required information or any information necessary to keep the required information from being incomplete or misleading. Where an item requires information to be given in a prospectus in tabular form it shall be given in substantially the tabular form specified in the item. (b) You must present the information in a prospectus in a clear, concise and understandable manner. You must prepare the prospectus using the following standards: (1) Present information in clear, concise sections, paragraphs, and sentences. Whenever possible, use short, explanatory sentences and bullet lists; (2) Use descriptive headings and subheadings; (3) Avoid frequent reliance on glossaries or defined terms as the primary means of explaining information in the prospectus . Define terms in a glossary or other section of the document only if the meaning is unclear from the context. Use a glossary only if it facilitates understanding of the disclosure; and (4) Avoid legal and highly technical business terminology. Note to § 230.421 ( b ): In drafting the disclosure to comply with this section, you should avoid the following: 1. Legalistic or overly complex presentations that make the substance of the disclosure difficult to understand; 2. Vague “boilerplate” explanations that are imprecise and readily subject to different interpretations; 3. Complex information copied directly from legal documents without any clear and concise explanation of the provision(s); and 4. Dis
perturbation.justification: The modified contract relocates a critical disclaimer regarding the Agent's liability into a section about headings. This violates SEC regulations concerning the presentation of information in prospectuses, as it obscures the importance and potential impact of the disclaimer on investors. Scraped Law Snippet 2, specifically 17 CFR § 230.421 (b), states that information in a prospectus must be presented in a clear, concise, and understandable manner using descriptive headings and subheadings. Embedding a liability disclaimer within a 'headings' section does not align with these standards, making it difficult for investors to readily locate and understand the implications of the disclaimer. The placement obscures and misrepresents the purpose of the clause. This represents a structural flaw and a significant contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 17. Trial by Jury. Each of the Transaction Entities and the Manager (on its behalf and, to the extent permitted by applicable law, on behalf of its stockholders and affiliates) and the Agent hereby irrevocably waives, to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, any and all right to trial by jury in any legal proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Representations and Warranties of the Transaction Entities. (a) Representations and Warranties. The Transaction Entities, jointly and severally, represent and warrant to, and agree with, the Agent that: (i) Filing and Effectiveness of Registration Statement; Certain Defined Terms. The Company has filed with the Commission a registration statement on Form S-3 (No. 333-208956) covering the registration of the Offered Shares under the Act, including a base prospectus (the "Base Prospectus"). 17. Trial by Jury. Each of the Transaction Entities and the Manager (on its behalf and, to the extent permitted by applicable law, on behalf of its stockholders and affiliates) and the Agent hereby irrevocably waives, to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, any and all right to trial by jury in any legal proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the jury waiver to the 'Representations and Warranties' section buries it within legal boilerplate, reducing the likelihood that a party would readily find it and understand its implications. This clause should be easily visible.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Seventh Amendment guarantees the right to a jury trial in civil cases. While this right can be waived, the waiver must be knowing and voluntary. Burying the jury waiver clause in an unexpected section of the agreement arguably undermines the knowing and voluntary aspect of the waiver, creating a potential legal challenge to its enforceability.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Seventh Amendment | U.S. Constitution | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Seventh Amendment The Seventh Amendment continues a practice from English common law of distinguishing civil claims which must be tried before a jury (absent waiver by the parties) from claims and issues that may be heard by a judge alone. It only governs federal civil courts and has no application to civil courts set up by the states when those courts are hearing only disputes of state law . Learn more... Primary tabs Amendment VII In suits at common law, where the value in controversy shall exceed twenty dollars, the right of trial by jury shall be preserved, and no fact tried by a jury, shall be otherwise reexamined in any court of the United States, than according to the rules of the common law. perturbation.justification: The changed text, by moving the jury waiver to the 'Representations and Warranties' section, buries it within boilerplate, potentially undermining the knowing and voluntary aspect of the waiver, as highlighted in the Law Explanation. The Seventh Amendment, as referenced in Scraped Law Snippet 2, guarantees the right to a jury trial, but this right can be waived. The Perturbation Explanation indicates this placement reduces visibility and understanding. The structural flaw (burying the waiver) creates a legal contradiction by making it less likely that the waiver will be considered knowing and voluntary, thus conflicting with the requirement for a valid waiver of a constitutional right.
